summary.narrative: Baldwin campaigned on inclusive governance, affordable housing, transit, jobs, and parks. Secured $39M for BRT transit; reformed community engagement (CAC system). Did not seek re-election in 2024. Limited public documentation of progress on affordable housing or job creation targets. Potential conflict of interest noted with TIG policy and developer contributions.
summary.grade_rationale: Transit initiative delivered with substantial funding; governance reform completed. Housing and jobs promises lack measurable outcomes or public documentation. Ethical concern flagged. Incomplete term prevents full assessment.

promises.normalized: Secure efficient, sustainable public transportation system
Provide affordable housing for all residents
Reform antiquated CAC community engagement system
Create good jobs and expand economic opportunity

data_gaps: No quantified metrics for housing units created or affordability targets
Job creation numbers and economic development outcomes undocumented
Parks and greenways expansion progress not detailed in sources
Campaign finance and TIG conflict-of-interest resolution unclear
Post-2021 policy accomplishments sparse in public record
Specifics on CAC reform implementation and community feedback absent
Departure timing (April 2024) lacks stated rationale or performance assessment
